notes: v2.0: added adventure/romance/family/wonder genre entries to structural_inversions (SI_16-23), sdt_wounds (SW_13-16), and world_textures (WT_16-21). v1.0 entries were exclusively institutional/thriller-biased.

sdt_wounds.description: denied the right to choose their own path by an institution they once believed in
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: Erin Brockovich $258M, The Report $limited-but-critical, The Insider $60M

sdt_wounds.description: chose freedom; the act of choosing destroyed every relationship that made freedom worth having
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: Marriage Story $limited, Blue Valentine, Frances Ha

sdt_wounds.description: built a system to protect others; the system now runs without them and produces harm
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: The Quota concept, Ex Machina $36M on $15M

sdt_wounds.description: excellent at the work; the institution makes their excellence invisible and assigns credit elsewhere
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: Hidden Figures $236M, The Favourite $96M, Whiplash $49M on $3.3M

sdt_wounds.description: mastered something the world no longer needs; the mastery is now the cage
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: Office Space cult, Up First 15 min resonance, The Full Monty $258M

sdt_wounds.description: did everything right; one variable outside their control destroyed everything they built
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: Parasite $266M, Nomadland $38M, I, Daniel Blake BAFTA

sdt_wounds.description: the more successful they become the fewer people in the world can actually reach them
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: The Social Network $225M, Whiplash, Steve Jobs $35M

sdt_wounds.description: knows something that if shared would end every relationship they have
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: Ordinary People $54M, Secrets and Lies, Hereditary $80M

sdt_wounds.description: lost the one person who saw them completely; is now invisible to everyone else
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: Manchester by the Sea $79M, A Ghost Story, Coda $limited

sdt_wounds.description: forced to be excellent at something they never chose; the excellence is the prison
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: Black Swan $329M, Whiplash $49M, Natalie Portman vehicle

sdt_wounds.description: to help effectively they must maintain distance; the distance is what prevents them helping
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: The Father $12M, Still Alice, Three Billboards $160M

sdt_wounds.description: denied autonomy, proof of competence, and any relationship where they are fully known — simultaneously
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: 12 Years a Slave $187M, Parasite $266M, Sorry to Bother You

sdt_wounds.description: separated from the ones they love by a choice they cannot take back — the wound is not loss but the knowledge that they caused it
born into a world that has already decided who they are — the wound is the gap between the life assigned and the life desired
loves someone they cannot keep without destroying what they have become — the wound is that both versions of themselves are real
sdt_wounds.commercial_proof: Titanic $2.26B, The Notebook, La La Land $446M

sdt_wounds.description: carries a power they did not choose and cannot fully control — the wound is that mastery of the gift requires abandoning who they were

psychological_patterns.description: person who mistakes precision for care — believes a perfect system is better than a present person
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: The Quota concept, A Beautiful Mind $314M

psychological_patterns.description: person whose identity was built around a role that is about to become obsolete
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Up first 15 min, The Full Monty $258M, Office Space

psychological_patterns.description: person who protects everyone by keeping everyone at a distance
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Good Will Hunting $225M, Logan $619M

psychological_patterns.description: the only honest person in the room — knows what is actually happening while everyone else performs not-knowing
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Spotlight $98M, The Report, Vice $76M

psychological_patterns.description: person who participated in harm because they were told with complete sincerity that it was good
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: The Quota concept, Hannah Arendt film, Son of Saul $3M art

psychological_patterns.description: still faithful to something that no longer deserves it — loyalty that outlived its object
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: The Remains of the Day $23M, Gosford Park $87M

psychological_patterns.description: the smarter they get the less anyone around them can follow — intelligence as a form of exile
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Good Will Hunting $225M, A Beautiful Mind $314M, Gifted

psychological_patterns.description: care that became control — started as protection, ended as captivity
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Hereditary $80M, Midsommar $29M, Sharp Objects HBO

psychological_patterns.description: did everything right and nobody noticed — achievement without witness
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Hidden Figures $236M, The Assistant, Never Rarely Sometimes Always

psychological_patterns.description: won't release the injury because releasing it erases the person it defined them as
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Manchester by the Sea $79M, Oldboy $15M, Prisoners $122M

psychological_patterns.description: has been right all along — the proof would destroy everyone they love
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Knives Out $312M, Gone Girl $369M, The Remains of the Day

psychological_patterns.description: brilliant at executing decisions they were never allowed to make
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Hidden Figures $236M, The Favourite $96M, Promising Young Woman $13M

psychological_patterns.description: the loss happened years ago — they are only now beginning to feel it
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Aftersun, The Rider, Past Lives $22M

psychological_patterns.description: not the injury done to them but the one they did — carrying their own betrayal
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Atonement $129M, The Reader $108M, We Need to Talk About Kevin

psychological_patterns.description: carrying damage that belonged to the generation before — inherited wound
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Moonlight $65M, Minari $16M, The Kite Runner

psychological_patterns.description: the true believer who discovers the foundational premise was false — built their life on a lie
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: The Truman Show $264M, They Live, Inception $836M

psychological_patterns.description: the more you master one domain the less you can perceive outside it — expertise as blindness
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: The Big Short $133M, Contagion $136M, Don't Look Up $limited

psychological_patterns.description: accumulated small decisions — the catastrophe didn't happen in one moment
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: The Zone of Interest, Ordinary People, Chernobyl HBO

psychological_patterns.description: their goodness is what makes the harm possible — good faith as mechanism of complicity
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Hannah Arendt, The Quota concept, Zone of Interest

psychological_patterns.description: the category that has no language yet — the event happened but the institution has no word for it
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: The Quota concept, First Reformed, 45 Years

psychological_patterns.description: person who encounters something that expands their understanding of what is possible — wonder that rewires their sense of reality
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Jurassic Park $1.1B, Finding Nemo $941M, Avatar $2.92B

psychological_patterns.description: person who discovers they have the capacity for heroism they never knew existed — courage emerging from ordinary circumstance
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Star Wars $1.98B, Harry Potter $1.34B, The Avengers $1.52B

psychological_patterns.description: person willing to give up everything they wanted because they love someone more — sacrifice as the truest measure of love
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Titanic $2.26B, Interstellar $675M

psychological_patterns.description: person separated from their family who will cross impossible boundaries to reunite — love as the force that defies obstacles
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Coco $814M, Finding Nemo $941M, Moana $645M

psychological_patterns.description: person born small who discovers they contain multitudes — transformation from self-doubt to self-knowledge
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Frozen $1.29B, Black Panther $1.35B

psychological_patterns.description: person carrying a curse who learns to see it as a gift — redemption through perspective shift rather than escape
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Beauty and the Beast $1.26B, Shrek $488M

psychological_patterns.description: person who thought they were the villain in someone else's story — discovering they are the hero in their own
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: The Dark Knight Rises $1.08B, Maleficent $758M

psychological_patterns.description: person who learns that home is not a place but a person — belonging discovered through loss and recovery
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: The Lion King $1.94B, Tangled $740M

psychological_patterns.description: person on a quest who discovers the real treasure was the companionship along the way — belonging earned through shared struggle
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Lord of the Rings $3.15B, Pirates of the Caribbean $1.15B

psychological_patterns.description: person transformed by confronting what they feared most — courage emerging through facing the darkness directly
psychological_patterns.commercial_proof: Inside Out $858M, Coco $814M

structural_inversions.description: the protagonist is the institution — not fighting the system but its most precise instrument
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: The Quota concept, Hannah Arendt

structural_inversions.description: no villain — the harm comes from a policy assumption, not a malicious person
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Spotlight $98M, The Report, The Big Short $133M

structural_inversions.description: the protagonist caused the harm before the story begins and is now discovering the extent of it
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Atonement $129M, The Remains of the Day

structural_inversions.description: antagonist and protagonist share the identical goal — they disagree only on what achieving it costs
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: The Dark Knight $1B, Michael Clayton $92M

structural_inversions.description: the investigation turns into a confession — started looking outward, ended looking inward
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Zodiac $84M, The Conversation, Chinatown $30M

structural_inversions.description: competence is the problem — the more effectively they do their job the worse the outcome
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: The Quota concept, Brazil, Dr. Strangelove

structural_inversions.description: the ending is neither victory nor defeat — the institution encounters something it has no procedure for
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: The Quota concept, First Reformed, Certified Copy

structural_inversions.description: the protagonist changes the world entirely by refusing to change themselves
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Gandhi $52M adjusted, Selma $66M, The Two Popes

structural_inversions.description: success reveals failure — achieving the goal proves the goal was wrong
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Whiplash $49M, Black Swan $329M, Perfect Blue

structural_inversions.description: the document or artifact is the real protagonist — the human created it and is now secondary to it
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: The Quota concept, Ex Machina $36M, Her $48M

structural_inversions.description: two stories that appear separate are the same story seen from opposite ends
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Arrival $203M, Memento $39M, Amores Perros

structural_inversions.description: the most dangerous person in the story is the one trying hardest to do good
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Hannah Arendt, The Quota concept, Three Billboards $160M

structural_inversions.description: the hero answered the call and the call was wrong — but they cannot un-answer it
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Apocalypse Now $150M, Full Metal Jacket, Deer Hunter $49M

structural_inversions.description: the world has no villain and no solution — only people doing their best inside a bad structure
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: The Wire (series), Nomadland $38M, I Daniel Blake

structural_inversions.description: the small act — not the dramatic one — is what changes everything
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Parasite $266M Joon-ho's detail focus, Shoplifters $11M

structural_inversions.description: the monster was protecting them all along — the source of greatest fear is the source of greatest love
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Beauty and the Beast $1.26B, Maleficent $758M, Monsters Inc $577M

structural_inversions.description: the hero's power comes from letting go — force creates failure; surrender creates victory
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Frozen $1.29B (Let It Go), Avatar $2.92B, Moana $645M

structural_inversions.description: the journey to save the world is actually a journey to find family — the external mission and the internal wound are the same thing
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Finding Nemo $941M, Coco $814M, Guardians of the Galaxy $863M

structural_inversions.description: they chose each other over survival — love is the irrational variable that defeats the rational catastrophe
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Titanic $2.26B, Armageddon $553M, Interstellar $675M

structural_inversions.description: the child teaches the adult what the adult forgot — wisdom flows backward through generations
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Up $731M, Inside Out $858M, The Lion King $968M

structural_inversions.description: the unknown world is not dangerous because it is alien — it is dangerous because it is a mirror of what they left behind
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Avatar $2.92B, Arrival $203M, Annihilation $43M

structural_inversions.description: the villain's cause is righteous — the only difference between hero and villain is which generation they were born into
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Black Panther $1.35B, Killmonger. Avengers Civil War $1.15B, Thanos $2.05B

structural_inversions.description: the ordinary person in the extraordinary world is the extraordinary thing — awe is not in the spectacle but in the witness
structural_inversions.commercial_proof: Jurassic Park $1.06B, ET $792M, The Hobbit $1.01B

compression_keys.description: one rule explains everything that seemed random — retroactive coherence
compression_keys.commercial_proof: The Sixth Sense $672M, The Usual Suspects $23M, Knives Out $312M

compression_keys.description: the method was causing the problem it was designed to solve
compression_keys.commercial_proof: The Quota concept, Brazil, Ex Machina

compression_keys.description: the protagonist was the antagonist all along — same person, different time
compression_keys.commercial_proof: Fight Club $100M, Gone Girl $369M, Primal Fear $56M

compression_keys.description: the cost was always known — the audience must now decide if they would have done the same
compression_keys.commercial_proof: Breaking Bad, The Americans, The Quota concept

compression_keys.description: the absence was always the presence — the gap in the record is the most important data point
the moment of maximum competence is the moment of maximum failure
compression_keys.commercial_proof: Whiplash $49M, Black Swan $329M, The Talented Mr. Ripley

compression_keys.description: what seemed like two separate stories is one story seen from two irreconcilable positions
the precision of measurement contains the exact thing it cannot measure
compression_keys.commercial_proof: The Quota concept, Oppenheimer $952M moral weight, Contact

compression_keys.description: the truth changes nothing — the revelation is complete and the world stays the same
compression_keys.commercial_proof: Chinatown $30M cult, The Zone of Interest, Hannah Arendt

compression_keys.description: the document that cannot be classified — the institution has no response category for what it received
compression_keys.commercial_proof: The Quota concept — genuinely novel narrative resolution

divisiveness_engines.description: the protagonist's central action was simultaneously right and wrong — the film refuses to adjudicate
the film forces the audience to answer a question about their own behavior before judging the protagonist
half the audience experiences the ending as victory; half as defeat — same events, opposite readings
the film takes a position in a cultural debate that has not been settled and both sides can claim it
the most sympathetic character causes the most harm — no clean emotional exit
silence as complicity — the film makes not-acting indistinguishable from acting
different audiences identify with different characters based on their own systemic position
divisiveness_engines.commercial_proof: Parasite $266M, Get Out $255M, Sorry to Bother You

divisiveness_engines.description: younger and older audiences reach opposite verdicts about the same ending
good faith is the mechanism — the more sincerely they believed, the worse the outcome
the systemic vs individual question — is this one person's failure or a structure's?

era_collisions.pole_a: human-scale decision speed (days, months, deliberation)
era_collisions.pole_b: machine-scale consequence speed (milliseconds, cascades)
era_collisions.bridge: a person trained to make careful slow decisions inside a system that now executes consequences faster than human perception

era_collisions.pole_a: analog era — one document, one copy, one place
era_collisions.pole_b: digital era — infinite copies, no original, no location
era_collisions.bridge: a methodology built around the scarcity of copies confronting a world where every document is simultaneously everywhere and nowhere

era_collisions.pole_a: generational knowledge transfer (decades, apprenticeship, lived experience)
era_collisions.pole_b: instantaneous knowledge access (search, retrieval, no context)
era_collisions.bridge: someone who spent a lifetime accumulating context in a world where context has been replaced by retrieval

era_collisions.pole_a: linear historical narrative — things happened, then other things happened
era_collisions.pole_b: simultaneous searchable past — all of history equally present and equally flat
era_collisions.bridge: a historian or archivist whose work depended on sequence now navigating a world without sequence

era_collisions.pole_a: biological lifespan as the unit of commitment (a career, a marriage, a promise)
era_collisions.pole_b: institutional lifespan measured in funding cycles, quarterly results, five-year plans
era_collisions.bridge: a person whose work requires decades to evaluate working inside a system that measures performance annually

era_collisions.pole_a: extractive linear economy — material enters, gets used, becomes waste
era_collisions.pole_b: regenerative circular system — nothing is waste; everything is input
era_collisions.bridge: an engineer trained in one paradigm redesigning infrastructure for the other — discovering the assumptions are architectural, not technical

era_collisions.pole_a: physical presence as requirement — the work must be done here, by this body
era_collisions.pole_b: remote execution as default — the work can be done anywhere, by any body
era_collisions.bridge: a craft or profession whose meaning was inseparable from physical presence now required to perform remotely

era_collisions.pole_a: scarcity as the organizing constraint — decisions made under assumption of not enough
era_collisions.pole_b: abundance as the organizing constraint — decisions made under assumption of too much
era_collisions.bridge: an institution designed for scarcity management now operating in a world of overproduction and disposal

era_collisions.pole_a: local food, water, and energy — what you needed came from where you were
era_collisions.pole_b: global supply chain — what you need came from somewhere you will never see
era_collisions.bridge: a community that understood its dependencies and a supply chain that made those dependencies invisible until they broke

era_collisions.pole_a: permanent material record — what was made, persisted
era_collisions.pole_b: ephemeral digital record — what was made can be erased, altered, or simply stopped being maintained
era_collisions.bridge: an archivist or restorer trained in permanence confronting the fundamental impermanence of born-digital artifacts

era_collisions.pole_a: Newtonian causality — every effect has an identifiable cause that preceded it
era_collisions.pole_b: complex systems — effects emerge from interactions; no single cause can be isolated
era_collisions.bridge: a practitioner trained to find and assign cause confronting a system where causality is genuinely distributed and emergent

era_collisions.pole_a: genetic determinism — your biology determines your outcomes
era_collisions.pole_b: epigenetics and plasticity — your environment rewrites your biology in real time
era_collisions.bridge: a scientist or clinician whose career was built on fixed biological categories now navigating the dissolution of those categories

era_collisions.pole_a: reductionist medicine — find the broken part and fix it
era_collisions.pole_b: systems biology — the organ is not the unit of failure; the network is
era_collisions.bridge: a physician trained in organ-system specialization now treating a patient whose condition crosses every specialist's boundary

era_collisions.pole_a: classical rational economics — agents maximize utility with available information
era_collisions.pole_b: behavioral economics — agents are systematically predictably irrational
era_collisions.bridge: a policy designer whose work was built on rational-agent assumptions now designing for an electorate whose behavior matches none of the predictions

era_collisions.pole_a: linear progress model — knowledge accumulates; problems get solved; things improve
era_collisions.pole_b: collapse and emergence model — complex systems fail catastrophically and reorganize at lower complexity
era_collisions.bridge: an engineer or planner trained in incremental optimization now managing a system that cannot be incrementally optimized — it can only be rebuilt

era_collisions.pole_a: authority-based truth — the expert says; the institution certifies; the public accepts
era_collisions.pole_b: distributed verification — truth is what the network agrees on after checking
era_collisions.bridge: an expert whose authority rested on institutional certification now operating in a world where the institution cannot certify faster than the network can dispute

era_collisions.pole_a: slow deliberative truth — evidence, peer review, consensus over years
era_collisions.pole_b: fast-propagating narrative — a compelling story reaches millions before the evidence is collected
era_collisions.bridge: a scientist or journalist whose methodology was designed for slow truth now competing with misinformation that moves at the speed of emotion

era_collisions.pole_a: quantifiable evidence — if it cannot be measured, it cannot be known
era_collisions.pole_b: qualitative lived experience — the most important things resist measurement
era_collisions.bridge: a researcher whose methodology requires quantification now trying to understand a phenomenon that only exists in the qualitative

era_collisions.pole_a: reproducible experiment as the test of reality — if it cannot be replicated, it is not real
era_collisions.pole_b: singular unrepeatable events as the substance of history — the most important things happen once
era_collisions.bridge: a scientist confronting a one-time catastrophic event that their methodology is structurally designed to ignore

era_collisions.pole_a: individual personal knowledge — I know because I was there, I experienced it
era_collisions.pole_b: institutional aggregate knowledge — the data says otherwise; the individual experience is noise
era_collisions.bridge: a person whose testimony contradicts the data — and the data is technically correct and completely wrong

era_collisions.pole_a: individual moral choice — I decide; I am responsible for what I decided
era_collisions.pole_b: statistical aggregate outcome — no individual decided; the outcome emerged from millions of small decisions that felt individually inconsequential
era_collisions.bridge: a person who made every correct individual choice and participated in a catastrophic collective outcome

era_collisions.pole_a: local consequence — what you do affects the people you can see
era_collisions.pole_b: global cause — what you do is determined by forces you cannot see and cannot locate
era_collisions.bridge: a community experiencing a local crisis caused by a global supply chain decision made by someone who has never heard of their community

era_collisions.pole_a: human lifespan as the unit of consequence — I will see the results of what I am doing
era_collisions.pole_b: geological or civilizational timescale — the consequences will arrive after everyone currently alive is dead
era_collisions.bridge: a person making a decision whose consequences they will never witness but whose children's children will not be able to reverse

audience_domains.name: adults with a chronic condition or dependent with one
audience_domains.entry_condition: the condition is named or recognizable in the story

audience_domains.name: institutional accountability drama subscribers (Spotlight, The Report, Erin Brockovich audience)
audience_domains.entry_condition: a system produces harm and someone tries to document it

audience_domains.name: women 25-54 who follow workplace inequality narratives
audience_domains.entry_condition: female protagonist navigating a system not built for her

audience_domains.name: adults who follow government and corporate accountability news
audience_domains.entry_condition: a public institution or corporation is under scrutiny

audience_domains.name: generational trauma community — people processing inherited family wounds
audience_domains.entry_condition: family wound is structural and crosses generations

audience_domains.name: working adults who experience systemic unfairness in their professional life
audience_domains.entry_condition: protagonist is competent and unjustly constrained

audience_domains.name: climate anxiety audience — adults whose daily life is shaped by ecological concern
audience_domains.entry_condition: environmental consequence is present even if not the main subject

audience_domains.name: mental health treatment community — adults with diagnosed conditions or in therapy
audience_domains.entry_condition: protagonist's internal experience is rendered with clinical accuracy

audience_domains.name: first-generation professional — first in family to enter a high-status institution
audience_domains.entry_condition: protagonist navigates a world with different rules than the one they grew up in

audience_domains.name: immigrant and diaspora identity audience
audience_domains.entry_condition: protagonist carries two irreconcilable cultural identities simultaneously

audience_domains.name: prestige drama awards-circuit audience (25-54 college-educated)
audience_domains.entry_condition: elevated storytelling, adult themes, awards-season release

audience_domains.name: healthcare workers and caregivers
audience_domains.entry_condition: the healthcare system's structure is visible, not just the clinical facts

audience_domains.name: LGBTQ+ community and allies
audience_domains.entry_condition: identity navigation within hostile or indifferent structures

audience_domains.name: science and technology professionals and enthusiasts
audience_domains.entry_condition: science is represented with accuracy and its social consequences are visible

audience_domains.name: grief and bereavement community — recent loss or anticipatory grief
audience_domains.entry_condition: loss is at the emotional center, rendered without sentimentality

audience_domains.name: young adults in identity formation (18-28)
audience_domains.entry_condition: protagonist is choosing who they will be, not who they have been

audience_domains.name: midlife reckoning audience (40-55) — questioning choices made at 25
audience_domains.entry_condition: protagonist faces the cost of their past choices when there is still time to act

audience_domains.name: economic precarity audience — adults whose financial stability is genuinely fragile
audience_domains.entry_condition: protagonist's material circumstances are visible and accurate

audience_domains.name: documentary and true-events audience
audience_domains.entry_condition: based on or inspired by documented events

audience_domains.name: philosophical and moral complexity audience — want films that do not resolve their questions
audience_domains.entry_condition: central question remains open at credits

world_textures.name: federal regulatory agency
intensive care unit
migration detention facility
algorithmic hiring system inside a corporation
climate research station in an endangered ecosystem
public defender's office in an underfunded court system
archaeological site where the past and present collide
elder care facility
a courtroom during a precedent-setting case
pharmaceutical research lab during a drug trial
archive or document repository holding suppressed records
international treaty negotiation
an AI development lab at the moment of an irreversible deployment
a village facing forced relocation due to climate or development
a prison where the guard and prisoner have irreconcilably connected histories
a living alien ecosystem with its own evolutionary consciousness
a family home across three generations — the same rooms, different decades
an ancient world being rediscovered — ruins that still hold living consequences
world_textures.commercial_proof: Black Panther $1.35B, Indiana Jones, Jurassic Park $1.06B

world_textures.name: a society in a single compressed moment of transformation — the last night before everything changes forever
a frontier world where the rules of civilization have not yet arrived — raw possibility and raw danger in equal measure
world_textures.commercial_proof: The Revenant $533M, Interstellar $675M, Dances with Wolves

world_textures.name: an enchanted realm where physical laws are expressions of emotional truth
world_textures.commercial_proof: Frozen $1.29B, Moana $645M, Beauty and the Beast $1.26B

civilizational_stakes.description: what is under threat is the shared method by which a civilization agrees on what is real
civilizational_stakes.commercial_proof: Applies to all $500M+ films with civilizational subtext — Oppenheimer, Interstellar, Don't Look Up

civilizational_stakes.description: a single methodology that held a civilization together for centuries is being tested for the first time
the speed at which the threat moves makes every human response structurally too slow
civilizational_stakes.commercial_proof: Don't Look Up $limited on Netflix, Contagion $136M

civilizational_stakes.description: the institution that was built to protect information integrity is now producing the misinformation
a single human practice — slow, laborious, and morally grounded — is the only thing that can distinguish truth from fabrication at scale
the adversary is not malicious — it is a property of the world that simply produces unlimited convincing falsehood as a byproduct of existing

methodology_protagonists.description: a verification methodology that took a human lifetime to build is the story's real protagonist — the human is its instrument
methodology_protagonists.commercial_proof: The Quota concept elevated — but here the methodology has civilizational scope

methodology_protagonists.description: a historical figure's lifework is being applied — without their knowledge — to a problem they could not have imagined
methodology_protagonists.commercial_proof: Genuinely novel — no direct precedent in theatrical corpus

methodology_protagonists.description: the method for authenticating truth was designed in an era when fabrication was difficult and slow — it has never been tested against fabrication that is fast and unlimited
the practitioner and the method have become inseparable — if the method fails, the practitioner ceases to exist as a person
methodology_protagonists.commercial_proof: Whiplash's obsession dynamic elevated to civilizational stakes

methodology_protagonists.description: the adversary has learned from the method itself — is using the verification logic to generate undetectable forgeries
methodology_protagonists.commercial_proof: Genuinely novel — adversary as student of the protagonist's method

methodology_protagonists.description: a chain-of-custody methodology built for analog objects is being applied to digital information — every assumption in the methodology breaks at a different point
methodology_protagonists.commercial_proof: Novel — no direct film precedent in this exact configuration

historical_methodology_transplants.methodology: trace evidence analysis — every contact leaves a mark
historical_methodology_transplants.modern_crisis: deepfake evidence that leaves no physical trace
historical_methodology_transplants.bridge: the foundational assumption of forensic science was that physical contact was necessary; in a digital world, harm leaves no trace

historical_methodology_transplants.era_of_origin: Enlightenment peer review and scientific method
historical_methodology_transplants.methodology: replication, consensus, and falsifiability as arbiters of truth
historical_methodology_transplants.modern_crisis: coordinated peer-review manipulation and AI-generated research
historical_methodology_transplants.bridge: the method designed to eliminate individual bias is being defeated by coordinated systematic bias

historical_methodology_transplants.methodology: community consensus verification — truth is maintained by collective witness
historical_methodology_transplants.modern_crisis: the community that maintained the tradition has been dispersed; AI generates synthetic versions
historical_methodology_transplants.bridge: truth that required a community of witnesses cannot survive without the community

historical_methodology_transplants.methodology: human intelligence network verification — every source confirmed by another source
historical_methodology_transplants.modern_crisis: every source is a potential AI-generated agent; the network cannot verify itself
historical_methodology_transplants.bridge: the tradecraft designed to resist penetration assumes human agents; algorithmic penetration is structurally different

historical_methodology_transplants.methodology: direct material inspection and master certification — a trained human examines the physical artifact, applies a guild mark, and stakes their professional reputation on authenticity
historical_methodology_transplants.modern_crisis: algorithmically predicted quality certificates issued before manufacture completes — the inspection happens before the object exists
historical_methodology_transplants.bridge: a quality auditor trained in the guild tradition of direct examination who is asked to sign off on predicted quality scores for objects they are not allowed to inspect
historical_methodology_transplants.commercial_proof: No direct precedent in theatrical corpus — under-used in the historical methodology transplant category

moral_fault_lines.description: loyalty vs truth — both are owed to the same person and cannot coexist
fairness vs care — the fair outcome destroys the person you care about
liberty vs protection — protecting someone means controlling them
individual conscience vs collective loyalty — one person's integrity vs many people's safety
efficiency vs dignity — the optimal system treats people as variables
present harm vs future prevention — doing harm now is the only way to prevent greater harm later
past justice vs future peace — pursuing the truth reopens a wound the community needs to stay closed
merit vs circumstance — the meritocracy rewards the person with the best starting position
scientific truth vs lived experience — the data says one thing; the patient's body says another
the debt that cannot be repaid — someone saved your life; you cannot save theirs back

formats.name: Feature Film
formats.description: Theatrical-first cinema; one self-contained story built for the big screen and a global box-office event.

formats.name: Limited Series
formats.description: A closed-ended premium streaming series (6-10 episodes) that tells one complete novelistic story.

formats.name: Returning Series
formats.description: An open-ended, multi-season streaming or cable drama built to renew and compound an audience over years.

formats.name: Animation Feature
formats.description: A theatrical animated feature; culturally legible worldwide, long box-office legs, strong family co-viewing.

formats.name: Animation Series
formats.description: A stylized animated streaming series for older audiences; fandom-driven, license plus ancillary (merch, games).

formats.name: Microdrama
formats.description: Vertical short-form serialized melodrama (60-100 one-minute episodes) monetized in-app on mobile.
